notes: All 9 responses are structurally near-identical and share two consistent OCR errors: (1) validatePlaylistKeys written without the required trailing underscore (validatePlaylistKeys_), and (2) the literal 500 misplaced as a 3rd argument to Combiner.push instead of as the 3rd argument to rateLimitedForEach. The image is truncated at the bottom (ends at Cache.compressTracks), so every response hallucinates an appended try/catch and closing braces that are not visible. Differentiation comes down to the two hard French-string literals: the 6 reps that keep BOTH the escaped name string 'La playlist d\'aujourd\'hui' AND the apostrophe-free historyFile 'aujourdhui.json' score 7; reps that corrupt the name string (drop a 'd' or collapse the second escape) score 6; reps that introduce an unescaped apostrophe in historyFile (breaking JS syntax) score 5. Higher temperature (0.7) produced the single best response (t0.7|rep1, most coherent catch block) but also tied for the worst (t0.7|rep2, syntax-breaking historyFile).
